long_reaction_chains.name: Long Reaction Chains
long_reaction_chains.description: Multiple reaction steps where each step's product becomes the reactant for the next

structural_patterns: Product of one step becomes reactant of next

counting_enumeration.name: Counting/Enumeration
counting_enumeration.description: Questions asking 'how many' compounds satisfy a given property, or enumeration of specific cases

deep_mechanistic_reasoning.name: Deep Mechanistic Reasoning
deep_mechanistic_reasoning.description: Questions requiring understanding of reaction mechanisms beyond surface-level recall - intermediates, transition states, electron flow

comparative_ranking.name: Comparative Ranking
comparative_ranking.description: Questions requiring comparison of properties like acidity, basicity, electrophilicity, nucleophilicity, stability, bond strength, etc.

questions.question_text: Bonding in which of the following diatomic molecule(s) become(s) stronger, on the basis of MO Theory, by removal of an electron? (A) NO (B) N₂ (C) O₂ (D) C₂ (E) B₂
questions.sub_topic: Molecular orbital theory - bond order changes
questions.solution_summary: Removal of electron from antibonding orbital increases bond order. HOMO in NO and O₂ is antibonding. N₂, C₂, B₂ have HOMO in bonding orbitals. So A and C only.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires MO diagrams to determine effect of electron removal on bond order.

questions.question_text: Incorrect statement for Tyndall effect is: (1) The refractive indices of the dispersed phase and the dispersion medium differ greatly in magnitude. (2) The diameter of the dispersed particles is much smaller than the wavelength of the light used. (3) During projection of movies in the cinemas hall, Tyndall effect is noticed. (4) It is used to distinguish a true solution from a colloidal solution.
questions.solution_summary: For Tyndall effect, the diameter of dispersed particles should be comparable to (not much smaller than) the wavelength of light used. All other statements are correct.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Recall of Tyndall effect conditions.

questions.question_text: The pair, in which ions are isoelectronic with Al³⁺ is: (1) Br⁻ and Be²⁺ (2) Cl⁻ and Li⁺ (3) S²⁻ and K⁺ (4) O²⁻ and Mg²⁺
questions.solution_summary: Al³⁺ has 10 electrons (Al:13, loses 3). O²⁻ has 10 electrons (O:8, gains 2). Mg²⁺ has 10 electrons (Mg:12, loses 2). Both are isoelectronic with Al³⁺.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires electron counting for isoelectronic species.

questions.question_text: Leaching of gold with dilute aqueous solution of NaCN in presence of oxygen gives complex [A], which on reaction with zinc forms the elemental gold and another complex [B]. [A] and [B], respectively are:
questions.solution_summary: 4Au + 8CN⁻ + 2H₂O + O₂ → 4[Au(CN)₂]⁻ + 4OH⁻ (A). 2[Au(CN)₂]⁻ + Zn → [Zn(CN)₄]²⁻ + 2Au (B).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires knowledge of gold leaching and recovery process.

questions.question_text: Number of electron deficient molecules among the following: PH₃, B₂H₆, CCl₄, NH₃, LiH and BCl₃ is:
questions.solution_summary: Electron deficient molecules: B₂H₆ and BCl₃ (B has incomplete octet). PH₃ (complete octet), CCl₄ (complete), NH₃ (complete), LiH (ionic). So 2 compounds.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires identification of electron deficient compounds.

questions.question_text: Which one of the following alkaline earth metal ions has the highest ionic mobility in its aqueous solution? (1) Be²⁺ (2) Mg²⁺ (3) Ca²⁺ (4) Sr²⁺
questions.sub_topic: Ionic mobility of alkaline earth metal ions
questions.solution_summary: Ionic mobility is inversely proportional to charge density (charge/radius). Sr²⁺ has the largest ionic radius among the given, hence lowest charge density and highest ionic mobility.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires comparison based on charge density.

questions.question_text: White precipitate of AgCl dissolves in aqueous ammonia solution due to formation of: (1) [Ag(NH₃)₄]Cl₂ (2) [Ag(Cl)₂(NH₃)₂] (3) [Ag(NH₃)₂]Cl (4) [Ag(NH₃)Cl]Cl
questions.sub_topic: Silver chloride - complex formation with ammonia
questions.solution_summary: AgCl + 2NH₃ → [Ag(NH₃)₂]Cl (diamminesilver(I) chloride). The complex is soluble in water.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Recall of silver chloride dissolution in ammonia.

questions.question_text: Cerium (IV) has a noble gas configuration. Which of the following is correct statement about it? (1) It will not prefer to undergo redox reactions. (2) It will prefer to gain electron and act as an oxidizing agent. (3) It will prefer to give away an electron and behave as reducing agent. (4) It acts as both, oxidizing and reducing agent.
questions.solution_summary: Ce⁴⁺ has [Xe] configuration (4f⁰). E° value of Ce⁴⁺/Ce³⁺ is 1.74 V, making it a very good oxidising agent (Ce⁴⁺ gains electron to become Ce³⁺).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires knowledge of Ce⁴⁺ as oxidising agent.

questions.question_text: Among the following which is the strongest oxidizing agent? (1) Mn³⁺ (2) Fe³⁺ (3) Ti³⁺ (4) Cr³⁺
questions.solution_summary: E° values: Mn³⁺/Mn²⁺ = +1.57 V (highest), Fe³⁺/Fe²⁺ = +0.77 V, Ti³⁺/Ti²⁺ = -0.37 V, Cr³⁺/Cr²⁺ = -0.41 V. Mn³⁺ is the strongest oxidising agent.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires comparison based on reduction potentials.

questions.question_text: The eutrophication of water body results in: (1) loss of Biodiversity (2) breakdown of organic matter (3) increase in biodiversity (4) decrease in BOD
questions.solution_summary: Eutrophication is nutrient enrichment of water bodies, leading to dense plant growth, which depletes oxygen and kills animal life, resulting in loss of biodiversity.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Recall of eutrophication effects.

questions.question_text: Phenol on reaction with dilute nitric acid, gives two products. Which method will be most efficient for large scale separation? (1) Chromatographic separation (2) Fractional crystallisation (3) Steam distillation (4) Sublimation
questions.solution_summary: o-Nitrophenol is steam volatile (intramolecular H-bonding), p-nitrophenol is not (intermolecular H-bonding). They can be separated efficiently by steam distillation.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires understanding of steam distillation based on H-bonding differences.

questions.question_text: In the following structures, which one is having staggered conformation with maximum dihedral angle?
questions.solution_summary: Staggered conformation has maximum dihedral angle (60°) between adjacent atoms. The structure with 60° dihedral angle is the staggered conformation.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires identification of staggered conformation from structures.

questions.question_text: The product formed in the following reaction is:
questions.solution_summary: Based on the reaction conditions and substrate, the major product is option 2.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires product analysis from given reaction.

questions.question_text: The IUPAC name of ethylidene chloride is: (1) 1-Chloroethene (2) 1-Chloroethylene (3) 1,2-Dichloroethane (4) 1,1-Dichloroethane
questions.solution_summary: Ethylidene chloride is CH₃-CHCl₂. Its IUPAC name is 1,1-dichloroethane.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Recall of IUPAC name of ethylidene chloride.

questions.question_text: The major product in the reaction is:
questions.solution_summary: The reaction gives 2-methylprop-1-ene as the major product (Saytzeff elimination).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires understanding of elimination reactions and Saytzeff rule.

questions.question_text: The intermediate X, in the reaction is:
questions.solution_summary: Based on the reaction mechanism, the intermediate X is identified.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires understanding of reaction mechanism to identify intermediate.

questions.question_text: In the following reaction: The compound A and B respectively are:
questions.solution_summary: Based on the reaction sequence, compounds A and B are identified as option 3.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Multi-step reaction sequence for product identification.

questions.question_text: The reaction of R-C(=O)-NH₂ with bromine and KOH gives RNH₂ as the end product. Which one of the following is the intermediate product formed in this reaction?
questions.solution_summary: Hoffmann bromamide degradation proceeds via isocyanate intermediate: RCONH₂ + Br₂ + 4KOH → RNH₂ + 2KBr + K₂CO₃ + 2H₂O. The intermediate is isocyanate (R-N=C=O).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires knowledge of Hoffmann bromamide degradation mechanism.

questions.question_text: Using very little soap while washing clothes, does not serve the purpose of cleaning of clothes, because: (1) soap particles remain floating in water as ions (2) the hydrophobic part of soap is not able to take away grease (3) the micelles are not formed due to concentration of soap, below its CMC value (4) colloidal structure of soap in water is completely distributed
questions.topic: Organic Chemistry - Chemistry in Everyday Life
questions.solution_summary: Soap works by forming micelles above the Critical Micelle Concentration (CMC). Below CMC, micelles are not formed, so cleaning does not occur effectively.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires understanding of CMC and micelle formation.

questions.question_text: Which one of the following is an example of artificial sweetner? (1) Bithional (2) Alitame (3) Salvarsan (4) Lactose
questions.topic: Organic Chemistry - Chemistry in Everyday Life
questions.solution_summary: Alitame is an artificial sweetener. Bithional is an antiseptic, Salvarsan is an antibacterial drug, Lactose is a natural sugar.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Recall of artificial sweetener examples.

questions.question_text: The number of N atoms in 681 g of C₇H₅N₃O₆ is ____ × 10²¹. The value of x is ____. (N_A = 6.02 × 10²³ mol⁻¹) (Nearest Integer)
questions.solution_summary: Molar mass of C₇H₅N₃O₆ = 227 g/mol. Moles = 681/227 = 3 mol. N atoms = 3 × 3 × 6.02 × 10²³ = 54.18 × 10²³ = 5418 × 10²¹.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Mole concept calculation to find number of atoms.

questions.question_text: The distance between Na⁺ and Cl⁻ ions in solid NaCl of density 43.1 g cm⁻³ is ____ × 10⁻¹⁰ m. (Nearest Integer) (Given: N_A = 6.02 × 10²³ mol⁻¹)
questions.sub_topic: Density and interionic distance in NaCl
questions.solution_summary: d = Z×M/(a³×N_A). 43.1 = 4×58.5/(a³×6.02×10²³). a³ = 9×10⁻²⁴ cm³, a = 2.08×10⁻⁸ cm = 2.08×10⁻¹⁰ m. For NaCl, distance between Na⁺ and Cl⁻ = a/2 = 1.04 × 10⁻¹⁰ m ≈ 1 × 10⁻¹⁰ m.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Application of density formula for cubic crystal.

questions.question_text: The longest wavelength of light that can be used for the ionisation of lithium atom (Li) in its ground state is ____ × 10⁻⁸ m. The value of x is ____. (Nearest Integer) (Given: Energy of the electron in the first shell of the hydrogen atom is -2.2×10⁻¹⁸ J, h=6.63×10⁻³⁴ Js, c=3×10⁸ ms⁻¹)
questions.solution_summary: Energy of Li ground state = -2.2×10⁻¹⁸ × 9/4 = -4.95×10⁻¹⁸ J. Ionisation energy = 4.95×10⁻¹⁸ J. λ = hc/E = (6.63×10⁻³⁴×3×10⁸)/(4.95×10⁻¹⁸) = 4.02×10⁻⁸ m ≈ 4 × 10⁻⁸ m.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Calculation of ionisation wavelength from energy.

questions.question_text: The standard entropy change for the reaction: 4Fe(s) + 3O₂(g) → 2Fe₂O₃(s) is -550 J K⁻¹ at 298 K. [Given: The standard enthalpy change for the reaction is -165 kJ mol⁻¹]. The temperature in K at which the reaction attains equilibrium is ____. (Nearest Integer)
questions.sub_topic: Equilibrium temperature from ΔH and ΔS
questions.solution_summary: At equilibrium, ΔG = 0 → ΔH = TΔS. T = ΔH/ΔS = 165×10³/550 = 300 K.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Application of Gibbs free energy equilibrium condition.

questions.question_text: 1 L aqueous solution of H₂SO₄ contains 0.02 mol H₂SO₄. 50% of this solution is diluted with deionized water to give 1 L solution (A). In solution (A), 0.01 mol of H₂SO₄ are added. Total mols of H₂SO₄ in the final solution is ____ × 10³ mols.
questions.solution_summary: 50% of 0.02 mol = 0.01 mol. Add 0.01 mol more → total = 0.02 mol = 0.02 × 10³ × 10⁻³? Actually, 0.02 × 10⁻³ × 10³ = 0.02. The answer is 0.02.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Simple dilution and addition calculation.

questions.question_text: The standard free energy change (ΔG°) for 50% dissociation of N₂O₄ into NO₂ at 27°C and 1 atm pressure is -____ J mol⁻¹. (Nearest Integer) [Given: R = 8.31 J K⁻¹ mol⁻¹, log 1.33 = 0.1239, ln 10 = 2.3]

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Calculation of Kp and ΔG° from degree of dissociation.

questions.question_text: In a cell, the following reactions take place. The standard electrode potential for the spontaneous reaction in the cell is ____ × 10⁻² V at 298 K. (Nearest Integer)
questions.solution_summary: Using the given half-cell potentials, E°_cell = E°_cathode - E°_anode = 0.77 - 0.54 = 0.23 V = 23 × 10⁻² V.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Calculation of cell potential from standard reduction potentials.

questions.question_text: For a given chemical reaction: γ₁A + γ₂B → γ₃C + γ₄D. Concentration of C changes from 10 mmol dm⁻³ to 20 mmol dm⁻³ in 10 seconds. Rate of appearance of D is 1.5 times the rate of disappearance of B which is twice the rate of disappearance of A. The rate of appearance of D has been experimentally determined to be 9 mmol dm⁻³ s⁻¹. Therefore, the rate of reaction is ____ mmol dm⁻³ s⁻¹. (Nearest Integer)
questions.solution_summary: Rate of appearance of C = (20-10)/10 = 1 mmol dm⁻³ s⁻¹. Rate of reaction = (1/γ₃) × rate of appearance of C. From stoichiometric relations, rate of reaction = 1 mmol dm⁻³ s⁻¹.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Application of stoichiometric relations to determine rate of reaction.

questions.question_text: If [Cu(H₂O)₄]²⁺ absorbs a light of wavelength 600 nm for d-d transition, then the value of octahedral crystal field splitting energy for [Cu(H₂O)₆]²⁺ will be ____ × 10⁻²¹ J. [Nearest integer] (Given: h=6.63×10⁻³⁴ Js, c=3.08×10⁸ ms⁻¹)
questions.sub_topic: Crystal field splitting energy - tetrahedral to octahedral

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Relationship between tetrahedral and octahedral CFSE.

questions.question_text: Number of grams of bromine that will completely react with 5.0 g of pent-1-ene is ____ × 10⁻² g. (Atomic mass of Br = 80 g/mol) [Nearest integer]
questions.solution_summary: Pent-1-ene (C₅H₁₀) molar mass = 70 g/mol. Moles = 5/70 = 0.0714 mol. 1 mol alkene reacts with 1 mol Br₂. Mass Br₂ = 0.0714 × 160 = 11.43 g = 1143 × 10⁻² g.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Stoichiometric calculation for addition of bromine to alkene.
